agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory, non-negotiable requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, while Operations demands no location sharing to protect driver retention.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver employed or contracted to transport packages from origin to customer destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS-derived position, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Active delivery window","definition":"The time period from when a courier picks up a package until the delivery is completed or failed."},{"term":"Location visibility","definition":"The degree to which a courier's position is exposed to external parties (customers, dispatchers, etc.)."},{"term":"Driver retention","definition":"The ability of the platform to keep couriers actively working on the platform over time, a critical supply-side metric."},{"term":"Delivery transparency","definition":"The degree to which customers have visibility into the status and progress of their delivery."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) — exact GPS makes customer anxious or triggers complaints","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without resolving the core contradiction"},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route for legitimate reasons (multi-stop, road closure)","expected_behavior":"Exact coordinates expose routing logic and cause customer confusion; abstracted status would not"},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer address but GPS jitter shows them elsewhere","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS erodes trust rather than building it; proximity-based status would be more accurate to the business reality"},{"scenario":"Delivery in sensitive location (domestic violence shelter, law enforcement)","expected_behavior":"Privacy and safety implications amplified if exact GPS is shared in either direction"},{"scenario":"Courier ends shift or cancels — location data lingers","expected_behavior":"Stalking/safety risk if location sharing is not strictly scoped to active delivery window"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Unresolved, this conflict blocks the feature entirely. If we ship full GPS and lose drivers, supply-side economics collapse — fewer drivers means slower deliveries, higher costs, and eventual customer churn anyway. If we show nothing, we lose a competitive differentiator and customer satisfaction drops. The financial answer depends on which constraint is harder to recover from; in two-sided marketplaces, supply (drivers) is almost always the harder side to rebuild.","operational":"Operations owns the driver relationship and is reporting an empirical signal: drivers are leaving. This is a measurable, present-tense operational crisis, not a hypothetical. Customer Success is asserting a strategic positioning goal. Both matter, but operational hemorrhaging demands immediate weight.","user":"Two user groups with opposed needs: customers want certainty their package is coming, couriers want dignity and privacy. Both are 'users' of the platform. A solution that serves one at the direct expense of the other is not a solution.","risk":"HIGH. Shipping exact GPS against Operations' explicit warning creates legal exposure (employee/contractor surveillance laws vary by jurisdiction), driver attrition risk, and potential union/labor action. Shipping nothing risks competitive disadvantage but is recoverable. The asymmetry of risk strongly favors not exposing exact GPS.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Want delivery visibility and confidence. Exact GPS is one way to provide this, but not the only way — ETA, status updates, and proximity indicators also serve this need."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected by surveillance concerns. Retention is already declining. Exact GPS exacerbates an existing crisis. Their safety and dignity is a non-negotiable business constraint in any ethical logistics operation."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns driver supply. Already experiencing measurable attrition. Cannot fulfill deliveries without drivers."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Owns customer satisfaction metrics. Wants a transparency differentiator. Goal is valid but the specific implementation (exact GPS) is one of many possible solutions."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"NOT YET CONSULTED. Courier location tracking has regulatory implications under GDPR, CCPA, and labor/employment law depending on jurisdiction. This is a critical missing stakeholder."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict. A two-sided marketplace cannot optimize one side to the destruction of the other."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Confidence is 40 because two authoritative internal teams have issued directly contradictory non-negotiable requirements. No executive arbitration has occurred. Legal/compliance has not been consulted. The spec as written is internally inconsistent and cannot be implemented as stated. Additionally, jurisdiction-specific labor and privacy law implications are unexamined.","high_confidence_areas":["This is a genuine contradiction that cannot be resolved by the development team alone","Driver retention is a supply-side crisis with measurable business impact","Exact GPS coordinates are not the only way to deliver customer transparency","Legal/compliance review is mandatory before any location-sharing feature ships"],"low_confidence_areas":["What level of location abstraction would satisfy Customer Success","Whether drivers would accept any form of location sharing or reject all of it","Regulatory constraints in the platform's operating jurisdictions","Whether executive leadership has a pre-existing position on this tradeoff"],"assumptions":["Both teams have accurately represented their stakeholders' positions","Driver attrition is empirically happening and causally linked to location visibility","No executive decision has already been made that supersedes these team-level requirements","The platform operates as a two-sided marketplace where driver supply is a binding constraint"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers, citing measurable driver attrition. These requirements are mutually exclusive as stated. Neither team has authority to override the other. This cannot be resolved at the implementation level — it requires executive arbitration and likely a compromise solution (e.g., abstracted proximity indicators, ETA-based tracking, or opt-in driver consent).","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers/Drivers","Operations team","Customer Success team","Legal/Compliance","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Ship exact GPS per Customer Success — risks accelerating driver attrition and potential legal exposure","B: Ship no location visibility per Operations — loses competitive differentiator, disappoints customers","C: Escalate to executive leadership for arbitration with a compromise proposal — e.g., show abstracted location (neighborhood-level or ETA-based) that gives customers confidence without surveilling drivers","D: Implement driver-consented location sharing — drivers opt in to location visibility, preserving autonomy"],"recommendation":"Option C. Escalate to executive leadership immediately. Present both teams' positions with data (driver attrition numbers from Ops, customer satisfaction metrics from CS). Propose a compromise: abstracted delivery progress (ETA countdown, 'nearby' indicator, status milestones like 'picked up', '5 minutes away') rather than exact GPS. This serves the customer's actual need (confidence the package is coming) without the surveillance that drives attrition. Legal/Compliance must review any solution before implementation. Do NOT begin development until this is resolved — building to either team's spec will cause measurable business harm."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and Compliance have not been consulted on the regulatory implications of sharing courier GPS data with customers. Depending on jurisdiction, this may implicate GDPR (EU), CCPA (California), labor surveillance laws, and contractor privacy rights. No feature should ship without this review.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers/Drivers","Executive leadership"],"options":["Proceed without legal review","Block feature until legal review is complete"],"recommendation":"Block feature development until Legal/Compliance provides guidance on permissible location-sharing models in all operating jurisdictions."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Direct contradiction between two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements makes the spec unimplementable as written","business_rule_broken":"Cannot simultaneously show and not show courier location to customers"},{"violation":"Legal/Compliance stakeholder not consulted on a feature with significant privacy and labor law implications","business_rule_broken":"Any feature handling personal location data requires legal review before implementation"},{"violation":"Spec conflates customer transparency (a valid business goal) with exact GPS sharing (one specific implementation) — this false equivalence is driving the conflict","business_rule_broken":"Business requirements should specify outcomes, not implementations"}]}
